choice: On a successful, complete (non-partial) sync of a mapped account, treat the returned position set as the authoritative snapshot for that account: a previously-held marketable Holding now absent is set to zero quantity as-of the sync date (position closed/sold), not left stale. This applies ONLY to marketable Holdings inside that mapped account, only on a fully-successful account return, and is recorded as a new zero observation (history preserved), never a delete. This is distinct from b45b7691, which forbids zeroing on failure/partial — here the account succeeded and genuinely returned no such position.
rationale: 777f97e5 makes sync authoritative; if a sold position stayed at its last synced quantity forever, net-worth actuals would silently overstate the portfolio — the exact failure mode b45b7691 guards against in the other direction. Scoping zero-on-absence to successful, complete account returns keeps b45b7691's fail-closed guarantee intact (a timeout/partial still touches nothing) while letting the authoritative snapshot reflect reality. Recording a zero observation rather than deleting preserves the actuals timeline the tracking area depends on.
